outbound_official.note: no official Gambia government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Searched mofa.gov.gm (Ministry of Foreign Affairs, International Cooperation and Gambians Abroad), gid.gov.gm (Gambia Immigration Department), and gambia.gov.gm (The Official Website of The Government of The Gambia). All official pages exclusively publish inbound visa requirements (who may enter The Gambia), not visa-free travel privileges for Gambia ordinary-passport holders traveling abroad. No dedicated outbound travel advisory or country access list was found on any official Gambia government domain.

Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'SCHENGEN/EUROPEAN UNION CITIZENS INCLUDING THE UNITED KINGDOM AND SWITZERLAND' and Section IV: 'Nationals of the 27 EU member states including Great Britain and Switzerland... can travel to The Gambia visa free'). Switzerland does NOT appear on the gid.gov.gm/visa/ enumerated list — sourced exclusively from the Gambia High Commission in Senegal official diplomatic document.

Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'UAE AND TURKISH CITIZENS'). CONFLICT: gid.gov.gm/visa/ places UAE under 'Countries That Need Both Visa And Clearance (Asia and Middle East)'. The HC Senegal PDF (dated Feb 2026) is more recent; it is an official Gambia diplomatic mission document. UAE status recorded here as visa-free based on the newer document. Travelers should verify directly with Gambia immigration before travel.

Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'UAE AND TURKISH CITIZENS'). Turkey did not appear in any category on gid.gov.gm/visa/. The HC Senegal PDF (dated Feb 2026) is an official Gambia diplomatic mission document. Travelers should verify directly with Gambia immigration before travel.

gambia.gov.gm states: 'Citizens of... full members of the European Union... do not require a visa to enter the country; whether on holiday or on a business trip not exceeding 90 days.' The Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'SCHENGEN/EUROPEAN UNION CITIZENS'; Section IV: 'Nationals of the 27 EU member states...') confirms this as a non-reciprocal waiver applying to ordinary, official/service, and diplomatic passports without requiring clearance.

gambia.gov.gm states: 'Citizens of... the Commonwealth... do not require a visa to enter the country; whether on holiday or on a business trip not exceeding 90 days.' Also confirmed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'COMMONWEALTH CITIZENS'). Individual Commonwealth countries not enumerated on these pages.

Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'AU CITIZENS') as visa-exempt. This encompasses all 55 AU member states. Note: several specific African countries appear in the 'Both Visa And Clearance' category on gid.gov.gm/visa/ (e.g. Sudan, Chad, DRC, South Africa, Egypt, Cameroon, etc.) — there is a direct conflict between the Feb 2026 HC Senegal document and the gid.gov.gm country list. The newer document is recorded here. Travelers should verify before travel.

Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'NORDIC STATES') as visa-exempt. Covers Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, Sweden (Finland, Iceland, and Sweden are also individually enumerated on gid.gov.gm/visa/).

Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'BALTIC AND BALKAN STATES') as visa-exempt. Baltics would include Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania. Balkans typically include Albania, Bosnia & Herzegovina, Bulgaria, Croatia, North Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia, Slovenia, Romania, Kosovo. NOTE: several of these (Albania, Belarus, Bosnia, Bulgaria, Croatia, Estonia, Georgia, Lithuania, Macedonia, Moldova, Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Ukraine) appear under 'Countries That Need Both Visa And Clearance For European Countries' on gid.gov.gm/visa/. Significant conflict with gid.gov.gm. The newer HC Senegal document is recorded here; travelers should verify.

visa_free.nationality: Tourists on Chartered Flights and Tour Operators (group label)
visa_free.notes: Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'TOURIST ON CHARTERED FLIGHTS AND TOUR OPERATORS') as visa-exempt, holding any passport type (ordinary, official/service, or diplomatic) per Section IV. gambia.gov.gm entry-requirement page notes tourists on last-minute bookings must submit passport to Immigration within 48 hours for a proper visa — this may apply to non-exempt nationalities.

visa_on_arrival.nationality: All nationalities (where no Gambian diplomatic mission is present in home country)
visa_on_arrival.notes: GID entry requirements page states: 'Visa can be acquired from all Gambian Embassies and some Consulates abroad as well as Visa upon arrival at the border.' gambia.gov.gm states: 'Where there is no Gambian diplomatic representative, participants shall have the privilege to obtain entry visa at the Banjul International Airport upon arrival.' Short visit visa valid up to 90 days. Note: countries that require both visa AND clearance must also have pre-approved entry clearance valid for 30 days before arrival.

visa_policy.notes: The official GID visa page (gid.gov.gm/visa/) divides countries into five categories: (1) ECOWAS free entry — 11 member states; (2) Countries with visa exemption agreement — 18 listed: Belgium, Finland, Poland, Sweden, Canada, Germany, Iceland, Italy, Luxembourg, Netherlands, San Marino, Spain, Tunisia, Indonesia, Russia, Australia, United Kingdom, Czech Republic; (3) Countries needing both visa AND entry clearance — African group: Sudan, South Sudan, Chad, Central African Republic, DRC, Congo Brazzaville, Gabon, Angola, South Africa, Lesotho, Cameroon, Mozambique, Burundi, Rwanda, Somalia, Algeria, Libya, Ethiopia, Djibouti, Eritrea, Madagascar, Comoros Island, Botswana, Equatorial Guinea, Egypt; European group: Albania, Belarus, Bosnia, Bulgaria, Croatia, Estonia, Georgia, Lithuania, Macedonia, Moldova, Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Ukraine; Asia/Middle East group: Afghanistan, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bahrain, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Brunei, Cambodia, China, India, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Japan, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kuwait, Kyrgyzstan, Lebanon, Laos, Mongolia, Myanmar, Nepal, North Korea, Oman, Pakistan, Palestine, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Syria, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, UAE, Uzbekistan, Yemen; Americas/Caribbean: Bahamas, Bolivia, Chile, Bermuda, Colombia, Haiti; (4) Countries needing only clearance — African: Kenya, Malawi, Mauritius, Namibia, Sao Tome & Principe, Seychelles, Tanzania, Uganda, Zimbabwe, Eswatini; Asia: East Timor, Indonesia, Malaysia, Maldives, Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Vietnam, Brunei Darussalam; Americas/Caribbean: Antigua & Barbuda, Barbados, Anguilla, Aruba, Dominica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Grenada, Guyana, Honduras, Jamaica, St Kitts & Nevis, St Lucia, St Vincent & Grenadines, Suriname, Trinidad & Tobago, Uruguay, Venezuela, Cayman Islands, Guadeloupe, Martinique, Montserrat, Netherlands Antilles, Puerto Rico; Pacific: Fiji, Kiribati, Nauru, Papua New Guinea, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tonga, Tuvalu, Vanuatu; (5) Countries needing only visa (no clearance) — African: Morocco, Swaziland, Zambia; European: Andorra, Austria, France, Liechtenstein, Malta, Portugal, Switzerland, Hungary, Romania, Greece; Americas: Argentina, Belize, Brazil, British Virgin Islands, Costa Rica, Cuba, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, USA; Pacific: New Zealand (and several Indonesian/Australian geographic regions listed in apparent error: Flores, Lombok, Melanesia, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Sulawesi, Sumbawa, Tasmania, Timor). A Feb 2026 'New Visa Policy' document issued by the Gambia High Commission in Senegal (official diplomatic mission) significantly expands the visa-exempt list. No e-visa or ETA system found on any official Gambia source.

conditional_access.conditions: Diplomats and Consular Corps are explicitly listed among visa-exempt categories in the Feb 2026 'New Visa Policy' document (Section I: 'DIPLOMATS AND CONSULAR CORPS'). Section IV states: 'Nationals of the 27 EU member states including Great Britain and Switzerland, Tourists on Chartered Flights and Tour Operators holding diplomatic, official/service or ordinary passports can travel to The Gambia visa free and are not required to be in possession of clearance.' Entry conditions: must possess valid travel document, enter through a recognised entry point, have visible means of sustenance, must not be a prohibited immigrant.

credential.subtype: Conventional Travel Document for Refugees (1951 Refugee Convention)

conditional_access.conditions: Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'HOLDERS OF CONVENTIONAL CARD FOR REFUGEES'). Entry conditions: must be in possession of a valid travel document, enter through a recognised entry point, have visible means of sustenance, must not be in the category of prohibited immigrants. Free entry for maximum of 90 days.

credential.subtype: Official travel document / laissez-passer from international organisation

conditional_access.conditions: Listed in the Feb 2026 New Visa Policy document (Section I: 'WORKERS OF INTERNATIONAL ORGANISATIONS SUCH AS; UN, AU, EU, IMF ETC' and Section II): 'Holders of official travel documents from the following international organisations can visit The Gambia visa-free: United Nations (UN) and its specialised agencies, African Union Commission (AU), ECOWAS Commission, African Development Bank.' Entry conditions: must possess a mission order or visa waiver; must hold official travel document or visitors pass/laissez-passer.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program exists. Per gid.gov.gm/citizenship/, citizenship is acquired only by birth, descent, marriage (after 7 years ordinary residence), or naturalization (after 15 continuous years ordinary residence). The 1997 Constitution of Gambia explicitly permits dual citizenship, though dual citizens cannot contest the presidency, National Assembly elections, or serve in the military.

agreements: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States) — free movement of persons

validation_notes: Re-validated 2026-06-13 against live official sources: gid.gov.gm/visa/, gid.gov.gm/entry-requirements/, gambia.gov.gm/immigration-and-visas/, gambia.gov.gm/entry-requirement/, and the Feb 2026 HC Senegal New Visa Policy PDF.

CORRECTION: Changed source_official from false to true on all three conditional_access entries. The Gambia High Commission in Senegal is an official diplomatic mission of the Gambia government; its policy PDF is an official government document.

ADDITION: Added Switzerland (CHE) to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I: SCHENGEN/EU including Switzerland; Section IV explicitly names Switzerland). Switzerland was absent from gid.gov.gm enumerated list and from the prior record.

ADDITION: Added United Arab Emirates (ARE) to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I: UAE AND TURKISH CITIZENS). This CONFLICTS with gid.gov.gm which lists UAE under 'Countries That Need Both Visa And Clearance'. Newer document recorded; conflict flagged in notes.

ADDITION: Added Turkey (TUR) to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I: UAE AND TURKISH CITIZENS). Turkey did not appear in any category on gid.gov.gm/visa/ in the prior record — it was only mentioned in data_quality.gaps.

ADDITION: Added 'African Union member states (group label)' to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I: AU CITIZENS). This conflicts with the gid.gov.gm country-level list which places many African countries under 'visa + clearance required'.
ADDITION: Added 'Nordic States (group label)' to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I: NORDIC STATES).

ADDITION: Added 'Baltic and Balkan States (group label)' to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I: BALTIC AND BALKAN STATES). Conflicts with gid.gov.gm which places several Baltic/Balkan countries under 'visa + clearance required'.
ADDITION: Added 'Tourists on Chartered Flights and Tour Operators (group label)' to visa_free, sourced from Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF (Section I and Section IV).
UPDATE: Added gambia.gov.gm/entry-requirement/ as an additional confirmed source URL (separate from /immigration-and-visas/ — both pages exist and are live).
UPDATE: Expanded source_urls list to include both gambia.gov.gm URL variants and the HC Senegal PDF.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: The 18 countries in the gid.gov.gm visa exemption agreement list are exactly as previously recorded. ECOWAS free-entry list (11 countries) confirmed unchanged. Mauritania/Mali/Niger/Burkina Faso privilege note confirmed unchanged. No e-visa or ETA system found. CBI/RBI/fast_track: no programs found, confirmed unchanged.
gaps: No official e-visa portal or ETA system was found on any Gambia government source.
No official RBI, golden visa, or investor residence program was found on any official source.
No official fast-track, digital-nomad, startup, or skilled worker visa scheme was found on any official source.

Indonesia and Brunei Darussalam appear in both the 'Countries With Visa Exemption Agreement' list and the 'clearance-only' list on gid.gov.gm/visa/ — an inconsistency on the official page; visa-exemption agreement entry is recorded in visa_free.

Several entries in the Pacific visa-only category (Flores, Lombok, Sulawesi, Sumbawa, Tasmania, Melanesia) appear to be Indonesian/Australian geographic regions rather than sovereign states — these appear to be listing errors on the official page and have not been recorded as separate entries.

The Feb 2026 HC Senegal New Visa Policy PDF creates major conflicts with the gid.gov.gm/visa/ enumerated list: (a) UAE listed as visa-exempt in PDF but as 'visa + clearance required' on gid.gov.gm; (b) 'AU CITIZENS' visa-exempt in PDF but many specific African countries listed as needing 'visa + clearance' on gid.gov.gm; (c) 'Baltic and Balkan States' visa-exempt in PDF but Estonia, Lithuania, Albania, Bosnia, Bulgaria, Croatia, Georgia, Macedonia, Moldova, Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Ukraine listed as needing 'visa + clearance' on gid.gov.gm. It is unclear which document represents current operative policy.

The exact nature of 'entry clearance' (whether a pre-arrival administrative permission or a form of visa) is not fully explained on official pages. The GID entry-requirements page clarifies it requires a host to take up immigration responsibilities in a prescribed form and must be obtained before travel.
mofa.gm (Ministry of Foreign Affairs) was not checked in this validation run (previously returned HTTP 403 Forbidden) — could not be checked for diplomatic passport waiver agreements.

No third-country credential-based conditional rules found on any official source — i.e., no rule granting entry based on holding a US, UK, Schengen, or other foreign visa, residence permit, or permanent residence card. No OCI cardholder rule found.
No specific bilateral diplomatic visa waiver agreements with named countries and dates were found on accessible official sources.

The gid.gov.gm/entry-requirements/ page still references 'valid PCR certificate within 72hrs of arrival' — a COVID-era requirement likely no longer enforced in practice but still present on the official page as of June 2026.

gambia.gov.gm immigration page states tourists on last-minute bookings 'will be allowed entry but will be required to submit their passport to the Department of Immigration in Banjul within 48 hours to be issued a proper visa' — the nationality scope of this provision is unclear.

The Feb 2026 HC Senegal PDF also lists 'SCHENGEN... CITIZENS' as visa-exempt in Section I, but the Schengen Area includes some non-EU states (Norway, Iceland, Switzerland, Liechtenstein) — the overlap with separately listed Nordic States and Switzerland creates ambiguity about exact scope.

France, Austria, Portugal, Malta, Greece, Hungary, Romania appear in gid.gov.gm 'Only Visa' (European) list but are EU members covered by the EU group label — these may represent an outdated list or countries that require an individual visa despite the EU exemption, the conflict is unresolved.
